guide.title: Use LDAP to synchronize people, not to replace the access model
guide.summary: LDAP fills the local cache of people and groups, but the platform still works with principals and memberships.
body: Configure one directory connection per organization when that organization manages people in LDAP.
After synchronization, review how LDAP groups map to organization roles and scopes.

concepts.label: LDAP Directory Sync
concepts.summary: The connector that imports users and groups from LDAP into the local identity runtime.
concepts.why_it_exists: The platform needs one maintained path for external directory integration while keeping access decisions consistent inside the application.
how_to_use: Configure the connection, base DN, bind account, and attribute mappings.
Run synchronization and review group mappings to roles and scopes.

relations.summary: Directory entries become local cached access identities.
Groups can result in organization access through memberships.
